title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 15 - Hire Those Allies! Send Those Henchmen to Check it Out!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DouSkzKX7NE

Daniel (@dungeondive) explores two of his favourite Shadows of Brimstone expansions: the Old West Allies and Feudal Japan Allies packs. These add hireable companions—blacksmiths, faithful dogs, henchmen, pack mules, and their Japanese equivalents (ashigaru conscripts, banner bearers, ronin bodyguards, and curious monkeys)—each available in basic or advanced form. Basic allies function as item cards with passive abilities. Advanced allies step onto the board as simplified heroes with their own stats, XP tracks, upgrade charts, and even mutation tables.

The highlight is the henchman's "check it out" ability: send your hired gun to investigate a darkness card, then roll on an exploration chart to discover their fate. A gruesome fate table delivers outcomes ranging from booby traps to possession by demon mind worms. Daniel sees these packs as proof of Flying Frog's deep, long-term commitment to supporting the game—a rarity in the dungeon crawl Kickstarter landscape. He especially recommends them for solo players running one or two heroes who need the extra muscle (or the extra donkey) heading into the mines.

----

Do you prefer sending henchmen to their doom or keeping your allies alive for the long campaign?
